title: Mira first-heart narrative delivery pilot
prompt:
Task #19

Work in:

C:\Users\robbi\Documents\Projects\newgame

Do not read archive/.

Run npm.cmd run handoff and confirm it prints Task 19. Work only on Task 19.

Objective: Implement one tightly bounded narrative-delivery pilot. When a successful delivery moves Mira the Baker from zero trust hearts to her first trust heart, show one brief inline authored payoff at the visible delivery surface. This tests whether a character moment improves the existing delivery loop without introducing a larger narrative system.

Inspect first: AGENTS.md, GAMEPLAY_ROADMAP.md, game-logic.js, app.js, index.html, style.css, game-logic.test.cjs, verify.cjs, service-worker.js, service-worker.test.cjs, release-check.cjs, package.json, and only other active files directly required by this task.

Required behavior: Define exactly one data-driven pilot for Mira (`customer-0`) at the successful 0-to-1 trust-heart transition, normally her third lifetime delivery. Use this exact content: kicker `MIRA · FIRST TRUST HEART`; title `A warmer morning`; body `Mira leaves a warm bun beside your coins. "Mornings are kinder with a friend."`; footer `1 of 3 trust hearts · New story ready in Journal`. Have the pure fulfillment result expose a small narrative payload only for that exact transition; do not infer it later from rendered state. It must work whether the triggering delivery is an ordinary order, Villager Special Request, or After the Stars order without replacing their existing completion results. It must not trigger before the boundary, for later Mira hearts, for other villagers, or for failed or repeated deliveries. Rerender, reload, and Starry Rebirth must not replay a first-heart payoff that was already earned. If Mira has not yet earned her first heart, a genuine zero-to-one transition remains eligible after Starry Rebirth, including when the qualifying delivery is an After the Stars order.

Present one non-modal code-native inline completion card at the delivery surface the player used: beside the Workshop ready-delivery flow for its shortcut and beside the Orders delivery flow for its button. It must be visible immediately without switching views, opening a modal, stealing focus, or requiring dismissal. Reuse the existing readable-then-collapse completion lifecycle and reduced-motion behavior rather than adding another timing system. Use a polite atomic live region. Preserve simultaneous Villager Special Request or After the Stars completion feedback.

Preserve relationship, Journal, and economy behavior. The existing 12-coin friendship favor remains unchanged. Mira's first Journal story unlocks through the existing heart rule but remains unread and claimable for its existing 5 coins, including its Journal notification dot. The inline payoff grants no currency, XP, invitation, keepsake, cosmetic, or progression and adds no saved field, migration, or save-version change. Keep the implementation small and data-driven; add no dependency, raster image, audio file, remote asset, or runtime service. If runtime files change, advance the existing service-worker cache identity exactly once and update only its matching automated assertion; do not change service-worker behavior or release documentation.

Explicitly out of scope: No content for the other eleven villagers or Mira's later hearts; no forced Mira order; no customer-selection or order-generation change; no dialogue engine, branching dialogue, village chapter, recurring modal, new navigation, new timer system, content framework, reward, economy, XP, level, Daily Goal, rolling-request, special-request, keepsake, After the Stars, rebirth, Workshop Look, tutorial, idle, Stardust, monetization, analytics, account, cloud-save, native-platform, or broader release work. Do not edit AGENTS.md, GAMEPLAY_ROADMAP.md, coder-tasks.json, README.md, release/readiness documents, or archive/. Do not commit or push. Do not start another task.

Acceptance checks: A deterministic Mira 2-to-3 delivery reaches one heart, retains the existing 12-coin favor, returns the exact narrative payload, unlocks Mira story 1, and leaves it unread and claimable. No payload is returned before or after that boundary, for another villager's heart, for an unsuccessful delivery, or for a repeated order ID. A genuine first-heart transition after Starry Rebirth remains eligible, while rebirth itself and already-earned first-heart state cannot replay the payoff. The payload adds no reward or persisted state. Existing commission and After the Stars completion payloads remain intact on the shared path. Rebirth friendship preservation, save compatibility, progression simulation, and economy behavior remain unchanged. Add focused browser-wiring or markup regression coverage without a DOM-test dependency.

Validation: Run npm.cmd run check. Exercise gather -> brew -> collect -> deliver -> upgrade in the browser. Use temporary deterministic browser state, not a shipped debug control. At 390x844 trigger the Mira payoff from the Orders Deliver button; at 360x740 trigger it from the Workshop quick-deliver shortcut. Confirm no modal, navigation, focus steal, horizontal overflow, browser console warning, or browser console error. Report coexistence evidence for existing reserved-order completion feedback if exercised.

Finish with exactly one overall status: PASS, FAIL, or BLOCKED, followed by Summary, Changed files, Validation evidence, Assumptions, and Deferred or out-of-scope findings.

title: Hostile save normalization and invalid active-brew recovery
prompt:
Task #20

Work in:

C:\Users\robbi\Documents\Projects\newgame

Do not read archive/.

Run npm.cmd run handoff and confirm it prints Task 20. Work only on Task 20.

Objective: Make current and migratable local-save normalization safe against hostile numeric values and reject a saved active brew whose known recipe is above the normalized saved player level. Preserve legitimate progress and all existing compatibility guarantees.

Inspect first: AGENTS.md, GAMEPLAY_ROADMAP.md, game-logic.js, game-logic.test.cjs, save-compatibility.test.cjs, release-lifecycle.test.cjs, economy-simulation.cjs, package.json, active save fixtures and frozen readers directly required for validation, and only other active files directly required by this task.

Required behavior: Treat every numeric field read from a save as untrusted, including numeric strings, negatives, fractions, non-finite-like strings, extremely large exponential values, Number.MAX_VALUE, and Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER. Normalize gameplay counters and values used by arithmetic or UI into finite, nonnegative safe integers with explicit domain-appropriate caps. Preserve existing smaller valid values and established caps. Level must remain between 1 and 10000. XP normalization must terminate in bounded work, never raise level above 10000, and finish in a canonical range below the XP requirement for the resulting level; preserve the existing reasonable multi-level-overflow behavior and remainder. Do not introduce an unbounded per-XP loop. Keep timestamps bounded by their existing rules.

For active brews, accept only a structurally valid known recipe whose unlock level is at or below the normalized saved level. Preserve a valid unlocked brew's recipe, timing, duration, and bounded assist use exactly as current normalization does. If the recipe is known but locked above the saved level, clear only the active brew. Do not raise level, unlock content, refund ingredients, grant a potion, mark discovery or mastery, award XP or currency, or reset unrelated progress. Preserve the existing safe rejection of unknown or malformed brews.

Keep future-version blocking byte-preserving and distinct from recoverable current or older saves. Preserve v1-v7 migration, frozen-reader rollback protection, save schema v8, storage-failure behavior, the four-hour offline cap, and deterministic gameplay/economy behavior. Prefer a small shared normalization boundary and focused tests; do not create a second save parser or broad recovery system. If runtime files change, advance the service-worker cache identity exactly once and make only the factual matching identity updates required by existing automated gates.

Explicitly out of scope: No storage acquisition/read/write/remove redesign; no manual-save or reset changes; no new warning modal or recovery UI; no achievement timing or lifetime-coin accounting change; no Daily Goal, idle-return, Stardust formula, order reward, recipe, upgrade, progression-envelope, gathering, narrative, Journal, After the Stars, tutorial, monetization, account, cloud-save, analytics, native-platform, or broader release work. Do not edit AGENTS.md, GAMEPLAY_ROADMAP.md, coder-tasks.json, README.md, release/readiness prose, or archive/. Do not commit or push. Do not start another task.

Acceptance checks: Deterministic adversarial tests cover the hostile numeric forms above across representative scalar counters and nested ingredient, potion, mastery, customer, order, daily, weekly, gather, stats, and brew fields. Normalization completes promptly, returns only finite safe bounded values, does not throw, and preserves valid neighboring progress. Reasonable XP overflow still crosses levels and preserves its remainder; extreme XP cannot hang or exceed level 10000. A known above-level active brew is cleared without any compensation or unrelated mutation. A valid unlocked active brew round-trips unchanged, including a completed-but-uncollected brew. Unknown and malformed brews remain safely rejected. Current v8 saves, v1-v7 migration, future-v9 overwrite protection, frozen-reader rollback checks, simulations, and the gather -> brew -> collect -> deliver -> upgrade loop remain unchanged. Save version stays 8 and no new dependency or saved field is added.

Validation: Run npm.cmd run check. In the browser, load a temporary adversarial current-version save without shipping a debug control, confirm the game opens responsively, then exercise gather -> brew -> collect -> deliver -> upgrade. At 390x844 verify safe recovery from an above-level active brew; at 360x740 verify a valid unlocked brew survives reload and can be collected. Confirm no horizontal overflow and no browser console warnings or errors attributable to normalization. Remove temporary QA state before final validation.

Finish with exactly one overall status: PASS, FAIL, or BLOCKED, followed by Summary, Changed files, Validation evidence, Assumptions, and Deferred or out-of-scope findings.

Level never exceeds 10000 and XP normalization is bounded while preserving reasonable overflow semantics
A known above-level active brew is cleared without compensation or unrelated progress loss
A valid unlocked active brew round-trips unchanged and remains collectible when ready
Unknown and malformed brews retain safe rejection behavior
